title: ParityKit Skill Message
description: Canonical envelope every skill uses to hand off data during a single agent run — to the orchestrating agent and to whichever skill runs next. Every message carries an explicit, typed confidence indicator; no skill hands off a bare result.

skill.description: Skill name, matching its SKILL.md frontmatter `name`.

skill_version.description: Semantic version of the emitting skill at the time this message was produced.

module.description: The targetComponent this message concerns — must match the input/artifacts subfolder and the knowledge-graph node.

run_id.description: Shared across every message in one agent run, so the full chain can be reassembled from output/reports/{module}-{date}.json.

status.description: 'blocked' = a required upstream input was missing or stale, not a failure of this skill's own logic.

value.description: 0.0-1.0. Not the final module confidence score — that's confidence-scoring's composite. This is how much THIS skill trusts its OWN output.

basis.description: Plain-language explanation of what this number reflects for this skill specifically — never a bare number with no basis.

source.description: calibrated = derived by formula from measured evidence (oracle coverage, sample size, prior run history). self-reported = a model's own assessment of its read (used only by ai-semantic-validation). Self-reported confidence must never be the reason a downstream score goes UP — see confidence-scoring/REFERENCE.md.

result.description: Skill-specific payload. Shape defined per skill in that skill's SKILL.md — see the 'Output message' example in each.

evidence_refs.description: Knowledge-graph node URIs this message relates to or produced (e.g. 'rule:PCHK-0003').

gaps.description: Explicit missing-evidence, ambiguity, or coverage-gap notes. A skill with nothing to report here still includes an empty array — omitting the field is not the same as confirming there are no gaps.
